facts: Brent crude futures spiked by 18%, currently trading at $112 per barrel.
Markets are pricing in a 60% probability of an extended, multi-week conflict.
The International Energy Agency (IEA) scheduled an emergency meeting to discuss the potential loss of 1.5M barrels/day if Iranian exports are heavily sanctioned.
OPEC members have signaled reluctance to immediately increase production quotas to offset potential supply shocks.
Defence sector equities and safe-haven assets like gold (up 4%) are rallying globally.
